installPromptBar.installHint: Save this website as an app to open the directory faster from your phone.

heading.title: Tech Communities
heading.subtitle: Directory of Spanish technology communities
heading.aboutCopy1: Our goal is to maintain the largest directory of Spanish technology communities. An initiative by
heading.aboutStar: If you find it useful, give it a ⭐ on
heading.bookmarkHint: And don't forget to bookmark it: {{shortcut}}.
heading.collaborateCopy1: New ideas, bugs and any other contribution are welcome —
heading.seekingPart2: to help keep local community data up to date.
heading.shareText: This {{handle}} directory shouldn't be missing from your bookmarks — it's the largest listing of meetups, conferences and tech communities in Spain: {{url}} (and it's open source & open data! :D)
heading.contributorsTitle: The community that makes it possible
heading.addCommunityTitle: Add a new community to the directory

tagSearch.placeholder: Filter by topic, audience or community…

communityCard.cbBadgeTitle: People who run this community are also part of the Community Builders community that drives this directory

app.checkingFreshData: Checking for a more recent version of the directory before opening details or suggesting changes.
app.syncingBeforeEdit: Syncing the latest version before allowing edits...
app.communityNotFound: I couldn't find the community you were trying to edit. You can return to the directory or open an empty form to create a new one.
app.unsavedChangesBody: If you leave now, what you've written in the form will be lost. You can keep editing, save a local draft in this browser, or exit without saving.

communityModal.archivedUrlNote: The main link points to an archived copy on web.archive.org because the original source no longer seems to be available.
communityModal.allTopics: Generalist community: covers any tech topic
communityModal.aiDataTooltip: Data enriched automatically, pending human validation. Under CC BY 4.0, your contribution will also benefit those who reuse this directory.
communityModal.cbTooltip: One of the people who leads or led this community is part of Community Builders, the meta-community of tech community organizers in Spain.
communityModal.shareMsg: This profile of {{subject}} summarizes what you'll find there at a glance: {{link}}{{cc}}

map.hiddenTooltip_one: 1 community matches the filters but doesn't appear on the map because it has no fixed location.
map.hiddenTooltip_other: {{count}} communities match the filters but don't appear on the map because they have no fixed location.
map.searchPlaceholder: Search for an address or place

taxonomy.searchPlaceholder: Filter by name, description or synonyms

urls.title: URLs
urls.description: Add the main URL and, if you want, more profiles or relevant links for the community.

primaryHelp.title: How to choose the main URL
primaryHelp.description: Use the most stable long-term reference for the community as the main URL.
primaryHelp.bullet0: Prioritize an own website or stable landing page over more ephemeral links.
primaryHelp.bullet1: If there's no own website, use the link that best represents the community and is least likely to expire.
primaryHelp.bullet2: Other profiles can be added as additional URLs.

urls.missingPlatformNote: If you're missing a platform type,
urls.openIssue: open an issue in the repository
urls.missingPlatformNoteSuffix: so we can keep improving the available options.

web.title: Website URL
web.description: Add the community's main website or most stable landing page.
web.bullet0: Examples: own website, official page or persistent microsite.
web.bullet1: If you've already used it as the main URL, there's no need to repeat it here.

eventsUrl.title: Events URL
eventsUrl.description: Link to the platform where they post events or tickets.
eventsUrl.bullet0: Examples: Meetup, Eventbrite, Luma, saraos.tech, Google Calendar or own agenda.
eventsUrl.bullet1: Use it when activity mainly lives on that calendar or platform.

linkAggregator.title: Link aggregator
linkAggregator.description: Link to a page that brings together several profiles or resources of the community.
linkAggregator.bullet0: Examples: Linktree, Beacons, Bento or similar page.
linkAggregator.bullet1: Useful when the community centralizes its presence in a single aggregator.

mailingList.title: Mailing list
mailingList.description: Link to subscribe or view the community's mailing list.
mailingList.bullet0: Examples: Google Groups, Buttondown, Substack or Mailchimp.
mailingList.bullet1: Add it if email is a relevant channel for following activity or interacting with other members.

github.title: GitHub
github.description: GitHub profile or organization used by the community.
github.bullet0: Examples: organization, main profile or central repository.

discord.title: Discord
discord.description: Invitation or access to the community's Discord server.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.description: Telegram channel, group or public link.
telegram.bullet0: Examples: `t.me/channel` or an official community group.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp
whatsapp.bullet0: Examples: `chat.whatsapp.com` invitations or links to public communities.

slack.title: Slack
slack.description: Workspace or access link to Slack.
slack.bullet0: Examples: workspace URL or official join page.

youtube.title: YouTube
youtube.description: YouTube channel or profile where they publish videos or streams.
youtube.bullet0: Examples: official channel, user or main playlist.

linkedin.title: LinkedIn
linkedin.description: LinkedIn page or profile associated with the community.
linkedin.bullet0: Examples: company page, showcase or official group.

twitter.title: Twitter/X
twitter.description: Main profile on X or Twitter.
twitter.bullet0: Examples: `x.com/user` or the community's official handle.

instagram.title: Instagram
instagram.bullet0: Examples: community's or event's main account.

facebook.title: Facebook
facebook.description: Official Facebook page, group or profile.
facebook.bullet0: Examples: fan page or public community group.

twitch.title: Twitch
twitch.description: Twitch channel where the community broadcasts or publishes content.

status.title: How to choose the status
status.description: Use the status based on the community's recent activity.
status.bullet0: Active: there are people running the community and it has had recent activity.
status.bullet1: Inactive: for meetups, usually applies if there has been no activity for more than a year; for conferences, if it has exceeded its usual cadence.
status.bullet2: Unknown: use it if it's unclear.

communityType.title: How to choose the type
communityType.description: Choose the main type that best describes how the community operates.
communityType.techMeetup: Periodic meetups, usually in talk format and contained size.
communityType.conference: Typically annual event, larger and with several talks or tracks.
communityType.umbrellaOrg: Groups local communities under a common brand or structure.
communityType.hacklab: Maker community with physical space and shared resources.
communityType.collaborativeGroup: People who collaborate on projects, events, content or other common initiatives.
communityType.metaCommunity: Organization that groups people who run or represent other communities.
communityType.mutualAid: The priority is mutual support through forums, chats, lists or other channels.

eventFormat.title: How to choose the format
eventFormat.description: Think about how the community's main activity usually happens.
eventFormat.bullet0: In person: they have in-person meetups and usually don't livestream them.
eventFormat.bullet1: Online: their activity is mainly online.
eventFormat.bullet2: Hybrid: there are in-person meetups and they are usually also livestreamed.
eventFormat.bullet3: Unknown: if you don't have enough context.

location.title: How to indicate the location
location.description: Indicate the most useful geographic reference for understanding where the activity happens.
location.bullet0: Use city or main region in a format like `City, Country`, for example `Barcelona, Spain`.
location.bullet1: Use `Itinerant` when the community changes city or venue depending on the edition or event.
location.bullet2: If you're not sure what value to put, it's better to open an issue to discuss it first.

parentOrg.title: What is an umbrella organization?
parentOrg.description: An umbrella organization is an entity that groups several local communities under the same network or brand (e.g. GDG Spain, PyLadies, Codebar). If your community belongs to one of these networks, select it here. The vast majority of communities have no umbrella organization.

shortDescription.title: How to write the short description
shortDescription.description: Summarize in one sentence what makes this community special.
shortDescription.bullet0: Indicate the organizational form if you know it: collective (without legal entity), non-profit association, foundation, cooperative, company with community activity, user group, etc.
shortDescription.bullet1: Prioritize what it offers, its focus or value proposition.
shortDescription.bullet2: Avoid repeating only labels, technologies or target audience that you've already marked above.
shortDescription.bullet3: An ideal length is usually between 140 and 220 characters.

deletion.reasonNotTech: This community should not be considered tech
deletion.reasonDuplicateMsg: The community is duplicated and should be consolidated into {{name}} ({{url}}).
deletion.reasonNeverExistedMsg: The community never existed as a real community and should be removed from the directory.
deletion.reasonNotTechMsg: This community should not be considered tech. See criteria and reference discussion: {{url}}
deletion.notActiveNote: If the community still exists but is no longer active, there's no need to request deletion: change the Status field to Inactive and submit an edit proposal.
deletion.notTechNote: The criteria thread about which communities we consider tech will be included as a reference:
deletion.otherReasonPlaceholder: Explain why this community should be removed from the directory.
deletion.helper: It will only be activated when you have provided a valid reason.

hero.descriptionEdit: Update this community's data in the form and click "Update" to create an issue in the repository.
hero.descriptionNew: Fill in the new community's data in the form and click "Suggest new community" to create an issue in the repository.

proposal.loaded: I've loaded the proposal included in this link. You can adjust it and reopen an issue with an improved version.

draft.restoredPrefix: I've restored a locally saved draft
draft.datasetChanged: The dataset has changed since then. Review carefully before submitting to avoid overwriting more recent changes.
draft.datasetUnchanged: No changes detected in the dataset since this draft was saved.
draft.legacyWarning: This draft predates the new metadata system, so the check may be incomplete.
draft.discard: Discard local draft and load the latest version

duplicate.warning: I found communities with a matching name or URL. Check if it's worth editing an existing one before continuing:
duplicate.byNameWarning: At least one community with this name or an equivalent one already exists:
duplicate.byUrlWarning: At least one community with one of these URLs already exists:

form.baseDataDescription: Main fields that make up each community in the directory.
form.langsHelp: Select the languages the community usually communicates or organizes in. If unsure, keep at least Spanish selected.
form.shortDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe in one sentence what this community offers, its focus or value proposition. Avoid repeating the tags or target audience already selected.
form.shortDescriptionExample: Example: "Non-profit collective that organizes monthly meetups to share practical experiences on software architecture and platform engineering."
form.thumbnailHelp: To replace the image you need a public URL. If you prefer, open the issue, upload the image there manually and comment that it should be used.
form.parentOrgTooltip: An umbrella organization is an entity that groups several local communities under the same network or brand (e.g. GDG Spain, PyLadies, Codebar). If your community belongs to one of these networks, select it here. The vast majority of communities have no umbrella organization.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Select tags from the catalog to improve discoverability and filters.
tags.suggestionText: Missing a tag or want to suggest an improvement to the taxonomy?
tags.matchesAllLabel: Generalist community — covers all tech topics
tags.matchesAllNotice: Checking this option means the community will appear in results for any tag filter. This implies applying all tags in the catalog:
tags.matchesAllWarning: Please make sure this accurately reflects your community to avoid creating false expectations for others.

audience.title: Target audience
audience.description: Indicate who the community is especially aimed at.
audience.suggestionText: Missing an audience profile or want to suggest an improvement to the taxonomy?
audience.matchesAllNotice: Checking this option means the community will appear in results for any audience filter. This implies applying all profiles in the catalog:
audience.matchesAllWarning: Please make sure this accurately reflects your community to avoid creating false expectations for others.

json.title: Generated JSON
json.description: This is the block that will travel inside the GitHub issue.

submit.note: GitHub will open with the issue title and body pre-filled.
